annotations.label: Wavelet (informal)

anchor.snippet: A wavelet is a wave -like oscillation with an amplitude that begins at zero

annotations.note: No notion of wavelet exists in Mathlib (loogle for "wavelet" returns 0 hits).

annotations.label: Middle C wavelet

anchor.snippet: For example, a wavelet could be created to have a frequency of middle C

annotations.note: A concrete musical wavelet example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet–signal correlation

anchor.snippet: Mathematically, a wavelet correlates with a signal if a portion of the signal is similar

annotations.note: Wavelet/signal correlation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet series representation

anchor.snippet: this representation is a wavelet series representation of a square-integrable function

annotations.note: Square-integrable functions exist as MeasureTheory.Lp but no wavelet series representation is formalized.

annotations.label: Huygens–Fresnel principle

anchor.snippet: the diffraction phenomenon is described by the Huygens–Fresnel principle

annotations.note: This physics diffraction principle is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet transforms as time-frequency representations

anchor.snippet: All wavelet transforms may be considered forms of time-frequency representation

annotations.note: Time-frequency representations of wavelet transforms are not formalized.

annotations.label: Uncertainty lower bound

anchor.snippet: The product of the uncertainties of time and frequency response scale has a lower bound.

annotations.note: No Heisenberg/Fourier uncertainty principle is present in Mathlib (loogle for "Heisenberg" returns 0 hits).

annotations.label: Three classes of wavelet transforms

anchor.snippet: Wavelet transforms are broadly divided into three classes

annotations.note: Classification of wavelet transforms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous wavelet transform

anchor.section: Continuous wavelet transforms (continuous shift and scale parameters)
anchor.snippet: a given signal of finite energy is projected on a continuous family of frequency bands

annotations.note: The continuous wavelet transform is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mother wavelet

anchor.section: Continuous wavelet transforms (continuous shift and scale parameters)
anchor.snippet: This subspace in turn is in most situations generated by the shifts of one generating function

annotations.note: No mother wavelet concept exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Examples of mother wavelets

anchor.section: Continuous wavelet transforms (continuous shift and scale parameters)
anchor.snippet: That, Meyer's, and two other examples of mother wavelets are:

annotations.note: Specific mother wavelet examples are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Child wavelets

anchor.section: Continuous wavelet transforms (continuous shift and scale parameters)
anchor.snippet: is generated by the functions (sometimes called child wavelets )

annotations.note: Child wavelets are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projection and wavelet coefficients

anchor.section: Continuous wavelet transforms (continuous shift and scale parameters)
anchor.snippet: The projection of a function x onto the subspace of scale a then has the form

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general orthogonal projection onto a subspace of a Hilbert space, but no wavelet-scale subspace projection or coefficient formula.

annotations.label: Affine system

anchor.section: Discrete wavelet transforms (discrete shift and scale parameters, continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: One such system is the affine system for some real parameters

annotations.note: The wavelet affine system is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for reconstruction

anchor.section: Discrete wavelet transforms (discrete shift and scale parameters, continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: A sufficient condition for the reconstruction of any signal x of finite energy by the formula

annotations.note: Frame/reconstruction conditions for wavelets are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiresolution analysis condition

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: this numerical complexity can be avoided if the scaled and shifted wavelets form a multiresolution analysis

annotations.note: Multiresolution analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Daubechies 4-tap wavelet

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: The most famous pair of father and mother wavelets is the Daubechies 4-tap wavelet.

annotations.note: Daubechies wavelets are not in Mathlib (loogle for "Daubechies" returns 0 hits).

annotations.label: Journe wavelet

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: the Journe wavelet admits no multiresolution analysis

annotations.note: The Journé wavelet is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subspaces from mother and father wavelets

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: From the mother and father wavelets one constructs the subspaces

annotations.note: Wavelet-generated subspaces V_m/W_m are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal complement W_m

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: W m is the orthogonal complement of V m inside the subspace

annotations.note: The orthogonal complement Kᗮ of a subspace is fully formalized, but the specific wavelet subspace W_m is not.

annotations.label: Refinement equation

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: is a refinement equation for the father wavelet

annotations.note: The wavelet refinement/dilation equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal decomposition of L2

anchor.section: Multiresolution based discrete wavelet transforms (continuous in time)
anchor.snippet: From the multiresolution analysis derives the orthogonal decomposition of the space

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the orthogonal decomposition of a Hilbert space into a subspace and its complement, but not the countable wavelet decomposition L²=⊕W_m.

annotations.label: Time-causal wavelets

anchor.snippet: it is essential that the wavelet filters do not access signal values from the future

annotations.note: Time-causal wavelet filters are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space L2(R)

anchor.snippet: This is the space of Lebesgue measurable functions that are both absolutely integrable and square integrable

annotations.note: L²(ℝ) is the special case MeasureTheory.Lp E 2 μ of Mathlib's general Lp space (with dedicated inner-product structure in MeasureTheory.Function.L2Space).

annotations.label: Zero mean and unit norm conditions

anchor.snippet: one can formulate the conditions of zero mean and square norm one

annotations.note: These wavelet normalization conditions (∫ψ=0, ‖ψ‖=1) are expressible but not formalized as a named statement.

annotations.label: Admissibility criterion

anchor.snippet: the mother wavelet must satisfy an admissibility criterion

annotations.note: The wavelet admissibility criterion is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Identity-representation condition (DWT)

anchor.snippet: one needs at least the condition that the wavelet series is a representation of the identity

annotations.note: The resolution-of-identity condition for wavelet series is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vanishing moments

anchor.snippet: to restrict ψ to be a continuous function with a higher number M of vanishing moments

annotations.note: Vanishing moments of a wavelet are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scaling and translation of mother wavelet

anchor.snippet: The mother wavelet is scaled (or dilated) by a factor of a and translated (or shifted) by a factor of b

annotations.note: The dilation/translation of a mother wavelet is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No basis in the CWT

anchor.snippet: there is no basis in the continuous wavelet transform

annotations.note: This statement about the CWT lacking a basis is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fourier transform as special case of CWT

anchor.snippet: the Fourier transform can be viewed as a special case of the continuous wavelet transform

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Fourier transform but no CWT, so this relationship is not formalized.

annotations.label: Wavelet time-frequency localization

anchor.snippet: wavelets are localized in both time and frequency whereas the standard Fourier transform is only localized in frequency

annotations.note: Time-frequency localization comparison is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: STFT kernel

anchor.snippet: one may think of the STFT as a transform with a slightly different kernel

annotations.note: The short-time Fourier transform kernel is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet energy via Parseval

anchor.snippet: Using Parseval's theorem , one may define the wavelet's energy as

annotations.note: Parseval's identity (and Plancherel via SchwartzMap.integral_inner_fourier_fourier) is formalized, but the wavelet energy definition built on it is not.

annotations.label: Temporal support of window

anchor.snippet: the square of the temporal support of the window offset by time u is given by

annotations.note: The temporal support spread of a window is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spectral support of window

anchor.snippet: the square of the spectral support of the window acting on a frequency

annotations.note: The spectral support spread of a window is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplication–convolution duality

anchor.snippet: Multiplication with a rectangular window in the time domain corresponds to convolution with a

annotations.note: Mathlib's convolution theorem (Fourier of a convolution equals the product of transforms) underlies this duality but is stated in the dual direction, not for the specific window/sinc case.

annotations.label: STFT time-bandwidth product limit

anchor.snippet: A given resolution cell's time-bandwidth product may not be exceeded with the STFT.

annotations.note: The STFT time-bandwidth limit is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DWT computational complexity

anchor.snippet: The discrete wavelet transform is less computationally complex , taking O( N ) time

annotations.note: Complexity analysis of the DWT is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthogonal wavelet via scaling filter

anchor.snippet: An orthogonal wavelet is entirely defined by the scaling filter

annotations.note: Definition of an orthogonal wavelet via its scaling filter is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Daubechies and Symlet via scaling filter

anchor.snippet: Daubechies and Symlet wavelets can be defined by the scaling filter.

annotations.note: Daubechies and Symlet wavelets are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadrature mirror filter

anchor.snippet: the high pass filter is calculated as the quadrature mirror filter of the low pass

annotations.note: Quadrature mirror filters are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet via wavelet and scaling function

anchor.snippet: Wavelets are defined by the wavelet function

annotations.note: Defining a wavelet via wavelet and scaling functions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Meyer wavelets via scaling functions

anchor.snippet: Meyer wavelets can be defined by scaling functions

annotations.note: Meyer wavelets are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet via wavelet function

anchor.snippet: The wavelet only has a time domain representation as the wavelet function

annotations.note: The wavelet-function-only definition is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mexican hat via wavelet function

anchor.snippet: Mexican hat wavelets can be defined by a wavelet function.

annotations.note: The Mexican hat (Ricker) wavelet is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet (mathematical function)

anchor.snippet: A wavelet is a mathematical function used to divide a given function or continuous-time signal into different scale components.

annotations.note: There is no definition of a wavelet in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wavelet transform

anchor.snippet: A wavelet transform is the representation of a function by wavelets.

annotations.note: The wavelet transform is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: DWT vs CWT classification

anchor.snippet: Wavelet transforms are classified into discrete wavelet transforms (DWTs) and continuous wavelet transforms (CWTs).

annotations.note: Classification of wavelet transforms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized transforms

anchor.snippet: There are a number of generalized transforms of which the wavelet transform is a special case.

annotations.note: Generalized transforms encompassing wavelets are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chirplet transform

anchor.snippet: Another example of a generalized transform is the chirplet transform

annotations.note: The chirplet transform is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fractional wavelet transform

anchor.snippet: Fractional wavelet transform (FRWT) is a generalization of the classical wavelet transform

annotations.note: The fractional wavelet transform is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gibbs phenomenon

anchor.section: As a representation of a signal
anchor.snippet: which is an observation known as Gibbs phenomenon

annotations.note: Mathlib only has Gibbs' inequality (KL divergence), not the Gibbs phenomenon of Fourier/wavelet series overshoot.

annotations.label: Wavelet denoising model

anchor.snippet: Suppose we measure a noisy signal

annotations.note: The wavelet denoising signal model is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shrinkage factor

anchor.snippet: is called the shrinkage factor, which depends on the prior variances

annotations.note: The denoising shrinkage factor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Haar wavelet

anchor.snippet: starting with Alfréd Haar 's work in the early 20th century

annotations.note: The Haar wavelet is not formalized in Mathlib (Mathlib has the Haar measure on locally compact groups, a different object).
